display.purpose: Tektur. Page-level h1/h2 headings only — never on buttons, nav labels, or measured values.

headline.purpose: Tektur. Component-level titles: CardTitle, score-panel section titles, mentor unlocked/locked headings.

body.purpose: Chakra Petch. All prose and every interactive control — buttons, nav items, form labels.

label.purpose: Space Mono, uppercase, 0.14em tracking. HUD-style section labels (eyebrows, category headers).

data.purpose: Space Mono. Anything computed or measured: scores, timestamps, slide refs, session IDs, wallet balance.

shadows.name: interactive-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus feedback on buttons only, never present at rest.

shadows.name: score-ring-glow
shadows.purpose: Score Ring's deliberate second exception to flat-at-rest — a CRT-phosphor glow in the score's own status color.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out-quint for all transitions and entrances. No bounce or elastic — unchanged from the prior system.

motion.name: rec-blink
motion.purpose: Literal VHS REC on/off blink, always paired with a REC text label.

motion.name: glitch-title
motion.purpose: One-shot title glitch on the sidebar wordmark only — never an infinite loop on a screen users stare at.

motion.name: boot-flash
motion.purpose: Color-bar boot flash in the app shell. pointer-events:none, skipped under reduced motion, does not replay on client-side nav.

motion.name: tracking-roll
motion.purpose: CRT tracking-bar sweep — reserved for one-shot triggers, never a randomized ambient interval.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA — Start Recording, Start Session.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #ff7a1a; color: #060d1a; font-family: 'Chakra Petch', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; padding: 12px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #ff8c3d; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4dd4cc; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #e06a0f; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Non-primary actions — Copy share link, live-feedback toggle label.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #0a1e35; color: #e8f4ff; font-family: 'Chakra Petch', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; padding: 10px 16px; border: 1px solid rgba(77,212,204,0.35); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #14304a; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #4dd4cc; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Score sub-category panel (Voice & Delivery, Body Language, etc), now with corner-bracket chrome.
components.html:
Voice & Delivery

Clear, steady pace throughout the talk.
components.css: .ds-card { position: relative; background: #0d2742; border: 1px solid rgba(77,212,204,0.22); border-radius: 8px; padding: 20px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Tektur', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.125rem; color: #e8f4ff; margin: 0 0 12px 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: 'Chakra Petch', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #e8f4ff; margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Topic, audience context, and other form fields on the Start Session screen.
components.css: .ds-input { background: transparent; color: #e8f4ff; border: 1px solid rgba(77,212,204,0.35); border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 12px; font-family: 'Chakra Petch', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; width: 100%; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9fb3c9; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #4dd4cc; }

components.name: Score Ring
components.description: Signature component — the hero overall score readout, now with a status-color glow.
components.css: .ds-score-ring { width: 160px; height: 160px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 8px solid #2ed573; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 6px #2ed573); display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-score-ring-value { font-family: 'Space Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; font-size: 4rem; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #2ed573; } .ds-score-ring-suffix { font-family: 'Chakra Petch', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #9fb3c9; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Live-feedback warning during a rehearsal session — message now renders in Space Mono as telemetry, not prose.
components.html: Speaking a little fast on slide 3.
components.css: .ds-toast { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; background: #0d2742; border: 1px solid rgba(77,212,204,0.22); border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; min-width: 240px; font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; color: #e8f4ff; animation: ds-toast-in 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } @keyframes ds-toast-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(40px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-toast { animation: none; } }

components.name: Corner Brackets
components.description: HUD-style corner accents lifted from the deck's .bracket motif — static, never animated, applied to cards/panels/the video frame.
components.css: .ds-bracket { position: absolute; width: 16px; height: 16px; border-color: #4dd4cc; border-style: solid; border-width: 0; opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; } .ds-bracket-tl { top: 0; left: 0; border-top-width: 2px; border-left-width: 2px; } .ds-bracket-tr { top: 0; right: 0; border-top-width: 2px; border-right-width: 2px; } .ds-bracket-bl { bottom: 0; left: 0; border-bottom-width: 2px; border-left-width: 2px; } .ds-bracket-br { bottom: 0; right: 0; border-bottom-width: 2px; border-right-width: 2px; }

components.name: Wallet Chip
components.description: Sidebar 'Tape Credits' balance — Space Mono number that briefly scales + flashes orange on increment.
components.css: .ds-wallet { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; border: 1px solid rgba(77,212,204,0.2); background: rgba(13,39,66,0.6); border-radius: 6px; padding: 8px 12px; } .ds-wallet-label { font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.625rem; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #9fb3c9; } .ds-wallet-value { font-family: 'Space Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 700; color: #e8f4ff; transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), color 0.3s; } .ds-wallet-value.increased { transform: scale(1.1); color: #ff7a1a; }

narrative.overview: OctoPrep2000's dashboard shares its visual identity with the Fuse Day pitch deck: VHS tracking, CRT scanlines, a blinking REC dot, navy instrument-panel chrome with one warm hero accent (orange) and a structural chrome accent (teal). This deliberately replaces the prior 'Recovery Score' north star (Whoop/Oura clinical restraint, no gamification, no bounce) — that system was right for a single high-stakes score screen with no surrounding context; once the dashboard grew a persistent shell (sidebar, membership tier, a wallet, an archive, a leaderboard) the brief shifted to match the deck's own showmanship. The bounce/elastic-easing ban is the one rule that survives untouched: Y2K showmanship here is confident ease-out, not springy.
keyCharacteristics: Two accent roles: teal is structural/chrome-only, orange is the single warm hero accent — never both as fills on one component.
Three typographic voices: Tektur for headings only, Chakra Petch for prose and every interactive control, Space Mono for anything measured or HUD-styled.
Static, cheap broadcast chrome (grid + vignette + scanlines + corner brackets) — no canvas redraw, no interval timers, safe to run through a live recording.
Choreographed motion (glitch, tracking-roll, boot flash) is one-shot only — never an infinite loop on a screen a user is actively working in.

rules.name: The Solid-Text Rule
rules.body: Decorative alpha-blended values (--dim, the vignette veil) are calibrated for a fixed-stage slide, not arbitrary UI backgrounds. Any text that must clear WCAG contrast uses a solid token (--pearl, --ash, a status color) — never an alpha value.

rules.name: The Two-Accent Rule
rules.body: Teal is structural (borders, focus rings, HUD labels, icons); orange is the hero (primary actions, brand, the wallet). Never both as fills on the same component.

rules.name: The Three-Voice Rule
rules.body: Display (Tektur) is for headings only. Body (Chakra Petch) is for prose and every functional control. Mono (Space Mono) is for anything computed or styled as HUD chrome. Functional text never renders in Tektur.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule (amended)
rules.body: No surface has a shadow at rest except the Score Ring's signature status-color glow — the one deliberate second exception.

rules.name: The No-Loop-On-Critical-Path Rule
rules.body: The recording screen gets static chrome only (corner brackets, a paired REC blink+label). No canvas noise, no randomized tracking-roll, nothing that runs continuously while a rehearsal is being captured.

dos: Do keep teal structural-only and orange as the single hero fill.
Do use a solid color for any text that needs guaranteed contrast — never an alpha value.
Do keep Tektur off buttons, nav labels, form labels, and data.
Do keep every status color paired with an icon or label.
Do keep chrome (grid/vignette/scanlines/brackets) static and cheap enough to run through a live recording.
Do label every pure-mock nav item (Leaderboard, Achievements, Settings) so the live demo never implies unbuilt functionality is real.
donts: Don't use bounce or elastic easing on any transition — this ban survives the pivot unchanged.
Don't run a continuous or randomized decorative loop on a screen the user is actively working in, especially the recording screen.
Don't use Tektur anywhere a user reads it as a control rather than a heading.
Don't use a colored side-stripe border on toasts, alerts, or archive/list rows.
Don't nest a card/panel inside another card/panel.
Don't make the score report's insight copy punitive or gamified in tone — that content stays factual even though the chrome around it is louder now.
